fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to delaware, respiratory therapist compact delaware, delaware respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Delaware Division of Professional Regulation — RT
board.mailing_address: Cannon Building, Suite 203, 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Dover, DE 19904

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Delaware.

steps.title: Create a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the DELPROS online portal at delpros.delaware.gov if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Log in to your DELPROS account, select 'Apply for a New License/Permit' from the menu, and choose 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' from the list of professions.

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within DELPROS, providing personal information, educational background, and examination details.

steps.title: Upload Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents directly through DELPROS, including official transcripts from an accredited respiratory care program and official NBRC exam scores. Request any required verifications from other states or institutions to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee (currently $117.00 for endorsement) online via credit card through the DELPROS system.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your DELPROS account for updates on your application status and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Delaware. To work in Delaware, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Delaware
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Delaware, navigate to the DELPROS online portal to create an account and begin your application.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DELPROS portal, click on 'Register' to create a new user account with a valid email address and password. Once registered, log in to start your application.
